title: C3: plan_context filter + review.modify canonical support + layer-flip auto-degrade
description: Extend plan_context to filter by relevance_paths: returns broad entries unconditionally + narrow entries whose relevance_paths any glob matches the input path. When no path arg, returns all (broad + narrow). fab_review.modify action extended to canonical entries (not only pending) with explicit support for relevance_paths / relevance_scope changes. Detect layer flip: if narrow team → personal in modify → auto-degrade scope to broad, clear relevance_paths, emit knowledge_scope_degraded event with reason: 'personal-implies-broad'. Anchors: plan-context.ts:226-254 buildDescriptionIndex + review.ts:74-106 reviewKnowledge dispatcher.

files.change: Add relevance_paths matching: include broad unconditionally; include narrow only when relevance_paths globs match target_paths input (use minimatch or fast-glob micromatch)
Accept canonical entries (not just pending); accept relevance_scope/relevance_paths in patch payload; detect narrow team→personal flip → auto-degrade + emit knowledge_scope_degraded
Add knowledge_scope_degraded event with fields stable_id, from_scope, to_scope, reason
Accept relevance_scope + relevance_paths fields in modify patch

implementation: Read plan-context.ts buildDescriptionIndex (lines 226-254) to understand current entry filter
Implement matchRelevancePaths(entry, target_paths): for narrow entries, use minimatch or globby to test if any relevance_paths glob matches any target_paths input string
Update planContext entry filter: if entry.relevance_scope === 'broad' include; if 'narrow' include only when matchRelevancePaths returns true; if no target_paths → include all
Add unit tests: broad always included; narrow with matching path included; narrow with non-matching path excluded; no target_paths → all included
Edit review.ts reviewKnowledge dispatcher: extend modify to accept entries from canonical dirs (.fabric/knowledge/<type>/ or ~/.fabric/knowledge/<type>/) by stable_id lookup

In modify handler: detect layer transition; if (oldEntry.relevance_scope === 'narrow' && oldEntry.layer === 'team' && newEntry.layer === 'personal') → set newEntry.relevance_scope = 'broad'; newEntry.relevance_paths = []; emit knowledge_scope_degraded event with reason='personal-implies-broad'
Add knowledge_scope_degraded event type to event-ledger.ts schema
Update api-contracts.ts review.modify schema to include relevance_scope/relevance_paths fields
Add unit tests for filter + modify-canonical + layer-flip auto-degrade

reference.examples: Existing modify-pending pattern in review.ts; description_index filter loop in plan-context.ts

criteria: plan_context filters by relevance_paths: broad ∪ narrow-matching-input-path
fab_review.modify supports relevance_scope + relevance_paths changes on pending AND canonical entries
fab_review.modify detects narrow team → personal flip and auto-degrades scope to broad, clears relevance_paths, emits knowledge_scope_degraded event
knowledge_scope_degraded event includes stable_id, from_scope, to_scope, reason fields
Unit tests cover filter (3 cases) + modify-canonical + layer-flip auto-degrade

rationale.chosen_approach: Filter logic in plan-context, auto-degrade logic in review.modify; emit explicit events for audit
decision_factors: personal knowledge crosses projects → paths inherently lose meaning
auto-degrade preserves user intent while preventing schema violation
rationale.tradeoffs: User loses original narrow paths after flip; mitigated by event log (auditable)

manual_checks: Manually invoke fab_review.modify with layer flip; verify auto-degrade + event

risks.description: Glob matching performance with large relevance_paths sets
risks.mitigation: Use precompiled matchers; benchmark with 200+ entries

key_functions.signature: function matchesAnyPath(globs: string[], targetPaths: string[]): boolean
key_functions.purpose: Test if any glob matches any target path
